rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad area (Gastown, Mt. Pleasant, Kits); center CA$2,750/mo; Gastown CA$1,800-2,500; mid ~CA$2,400 (~$1,750 USD)

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; BC Hydro moderate; internet CA$70-80/mo

groceries.note: home cooking; Superstore/Save-On/T&T; CA$355/mo reported; Vancouver grocery costs elevated

diningOut.note: Vancouver excellent food scene; lunch ~CA$15-20, dinner CA$25-45; ramen/sushi affordable

transit.note: TransLink monthly Compass Card ~CA$115 (~$85); good Skytrain network

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; budget options from CA$50 (FreeSpace); L'atelier unlimited $200 USD; mid ~CA$250

simData.note: SIM-only plan (Koodo/Public Mobile/Lucky); Canada mobile notoriously expensive; unlimited ~CA$45-60/mo

misc.note: gym (~CA$50), toiletries, entertainment, outdoor recreation (hiking, skiing), buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Vancouver medium-term furnished lets are fairly stable year-round.

evidence.claim: Vancouver 1BR rent city center vs Gastown
evidence.quote: A one-bedroom apartment in Vancouver's city center costs around CA$2,750 per month; in Gastown, rent for a one-bedroom apartment can range from $1,800 to $2,500 CAD ($1,400 to $1,900 USD) per month
you should expect to spend about $355 monthly on groceries
FreeSpace is only $49.99 per month; L'atelier Vancouver monthly unlimited access to hot desk for 200 USD
a full monthly budget (housing + food/transport) runs roughly C$3,000–4,200 in Vancouver
